prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, a CPU is like the brain of the computer, sort of like how your head helps you think and decide what to do. It’s a little box inside the machine that works very hard, figuring out all the instructions so the computer can play games, show videos, or let you write your school report. I imagine it like a busy little postmaster, sorting through letters and sending them where they need to go, only it’s sending tiny bits of information instead. It doesn’t get tired, even though it zips things around super fast—faster than a hummingbird’s wing! We can’t see it working, but everything on the screen happens because of it. Isn’t that something?
